This classification is based on the unique features and characteristics of the species, including its eight-legged body and ability to withstand extreme environmental conditions. Calpidisca hispida is further classified within the family Macrobiotidae, which is a group of large water bears. The family Macrobiotidae is part of the order Eutardigrada, which is a group of eutardigrades that have a more advanced body structure compared to other tardigrades. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Calpidisca hispida within the larger group of tardigrades.
